title: Where Traffic's Engine-Sound Becomes Wind Between Grass

features.name: engine-frequency-trace
features.desc-on-touch: the highway's roar rendered as waveform, then the waveform learned to blow like wind through a field. now you cannot hear which is which.

features.name: paddock-rendered-circuit
features.desc-on-touch: grass plotted on a grid, each blade a coordinate, each wind-gust a signal. the highway forgot it was a road and remembered it was a space between things.

features.name: diagnostic-grass-tower
features.desc-on-touch: a tower of rendered wind-bent grass, standing in the coordinate system like a reproof. the archive measured the paddock and found it was never empty.

features.name: silence-frequency-marker
features.desc-on-touch: the moment the engine-sound became wind — plotted exactly, time-stamped. the archive has never been certain whether it was listening or dreaming.

features.name: layby-grass-node-register
features.desc-on-touch: a small monument: this is where traffic learned to rest. not to move, but to pause. the grass held the space open. the wind kept it cool.

inscription: the highway whispered to the paddock: i was built to move, but i am happiest when nothing moves along me. the grass said: i am happiest blowing.

file.name: Layby_Wind_Survey_Acoustic_1994-2001.mp3
file.note: 7 years of hourly wind recordings at the layby where the highway straightens. gradually the file learned to include vehicle noise. by year 7, the two were indistinguishable.

exits.label: east, back to the layby where journeys still pause
west into paddock country, where grids dissolve into fencelines
exits.to: NEW: the open country where coordinates forget themselves

exits.label: north toward where the grid becomes sparse and sky opens
south toward the horizon where wind and traffic both disappear
exits.to: NEW: where the grid flattens into silence
